messages.content:
i need to write a manifesto of beauty script based on these instrctions: The foyer of your museum will house your manifesto of beauty for all to see.  In it, you will define the concept of beauty as you see it.  It will dictate the legitimacy of the aesthetic judgments you’ve made for each of your museum’s four wings (Fine Art: music album)(Natural Beauty, provincial park)(Human Beauty, Sports player)(Everyday beauty, my bed)
In a minimum of 100 words, create a position piece that provides your personal definition of beauty.  The best manifestos will reference the works of famous philosophers and will give all-encompassing definitions that touch on some of the key terms discussed in class and in the readings.
use this concept below (my view or maifesto) and create the script, dont make the vocab too complex: A very common element in many conceptions of beauty is its relation to pleasure.[11][5] Hedonism makes this relation part of the definition of beauty by holding that there is a necessary connection between pleasure and beauty, e.g. that for an object to be beautiful is for it to cause pleasure or that the experience of beauty is always accompanied by pleasure.[12] This account is sometimes labeled as "aesthetic hedonism" in order to distinguish it from other forms of hedonism.

Manifesto of Beauty: An Ode to Aesthetic Hedonism

As one steps into the sanctum of this museum, they are invited to explore a realm where beauty transcends mere appearance, delving into the profound connection it shares with pleasure. This manifesto heralds a vision of beauty shaped by a principle that is both timeless and universal - aesthetic hedonism. It posits that the core of beauty lies not just in the external form, but in the joy and the profound satisfaction it evokes within the human spirit.

In the echoing corridors of the Fine Art wing, where melodies and harmonies are suspended in time, we witness the embodiment of beauty as a source of pleasure. The euphoria elicited by a music album, the meticulous arrangement of notes and rhythms, underscores the intrinsic link between auditory beauty and the pleasure it furnishes, much like the philosophical musings of Immanuel Kant who contemplated beauty as an object of delight.

Venturing into the Natural Beauty wing, we are enveloped by the grandeur of provincial parks. Herein, beauty engulfs us in its most pristine form - unraveled, vast, and majestic. This natural beauty, untouched and pure, offers a solace and a pleasure so profound, aligning with John Keats’ assertion that ‘a thing of beauty is a joy forever’. It reinforces our belief that beauty in nature is an inexhaustible source of pleasure.

Turning our gaze to the Human Beauty wing, we celebrate the athletic grace of a sports player. This beauty, characterized by physical prowess and the euphoria of human achievement, mirrors the Aristotelian ideal of beauty harmonized with function and excellence. The admiration and pleasure derived from witnessing human potential translated into performance are unparalleled, revealing the multifaceted essence of beauty as both an inspiration and a source of joy.

Lastly, when we retreat to the domain of Everyday Beauty, epitomized by something as intimate as ‘my bed’, we encounter beauty in its most personal and comforting form. This everyday beauty, woven into the fabric of our daily lives, invites us to find joy in simplicity and serenity. Echoing the sentiments of William Morris who championed beauty in the realms of the ordinary, this wing celebrates the gentle pleasures derived from the familiar and the cherished.

Thus, through the lenses of aesthetic hedonism, this museum and its manifesto strive to illuminate the inseparable bond between beauty and pleasure. It is a testament to the belief that beauty, in its myriad manifestations, is a perennial source of pleasure and joy. Let this manifesto be a guide, inviting all who enter to discover, delight in, and celebrate the profound and varied joys that beauty, in its infinite forms, bestows upon the human experience.
make sure its only 100 words

In the heart of this museum lies a manifesto: beauty is the pursuit of pleasure, a principle echoing the ethos of aesthetic hedonism. From the euphoric melodies within the Fine Art wing to the serene grandeur of Natural Beauty, from the awe-inspiring grace of a celebrated Sports player to the comforting embrace of Everyday Beauty, each wing validates beauty's intrinsic link to joy. Drawing inspiration from philosophers like Kant and Keats, this manifesto champions the notion that beauty, in all its forms, serves as a conduit to pleasure, enriching the human experience with unfading delight and satisfaction.
simplify the vocabulary A LITTLE BIT

At the heart of our museum, we declare: beauty is all about finding joy. This idea, based on aesthetic hedonism, shows that whether it's the thrill of music in our Fine Art wing, the calm beauty of nature, the remarkable skill of a sports player, or the simple comfort found in everyday things like your bed, beauty’s true value lies in the happiness it brings. Inspired by thinkers like Kant and Keats, our manifesto celebrates that beauty, in every form, is a path to joy. It enriches our lives, reminding us to cherish the pleasure found in the world around us.
instead of saying what beauty brings, define it.

Our museum stands on a simple yet profound declaration: beauty is joy personified. Rooted in the philosophy of aesthetic hedonism, this manifesto defines beauty not just by what it brings or how it appears, but by its essence of sparking joy. Whether it's the immersive melodies in our Fine Art wing, the serene vistas of our Natural Beauty section, the dynamic display of athleticism in the Human Beauty exhibit, or the comforting familiarity found in everyday sights, like one's bed, each element embodies beauty by being a source of happiness. Drawing inspiration from thinkers like Kant and Keats, we see beauty as the embodiment of joy, a core aspect that makes life richer and more pleasurable.
simply this a little as it is a presentation

Our museum's message is clear: Beauty equals joy. This belief comes from the idea of aesthetic hedonism, which tells us that beauty is fundamentally about creating happiness. So, whether we're talking about the moving music in our Fine Art section, the peaceful nature scenes, the energy of a great sports performance, or the simple comfort of something like a bed, all these forms of beauty share one thing - they make us happy. Inspired by great thinkers like Kant and Keats, our manifesto views beauty as happiness made visible, enriching our lives in the most delightful way.

SIMPLIFY THE FOLLOWING TO MAKE IT 100-150 WORDS AND MAKE IT SOUND HUMAN AND LESS LIKE A ROBOT, BECAUSE I AM PRESENTING THIS. STILL KEEP IT FORMAL.Beauty is a complex idea that goes beyond art, nature, and human experience. It is a special way of communicating that touches the heart, bringing out emotions and exciting the senses. In my view, beauty is not just a surface-level quality but a reflection of the natural balance and harmony that exists in the world.
The core of my definition of beauty is its connection to pleasure. Like aesthetic hedonism, I believe beauty is inherently linked to the joy it brings. When we see a beautiful painting, a stunning landscape, or a kind act, the experience of beauty is accompanied by happiness, awe, and contentment. This pleasure is not just on the surface but a deep feeling with the essence of what we find beautiful.
However, beauty is more than just pleasurable. It can also evoke deeper feelings, such as thought, self-reflection, and even a sense of wonder. A work of art that challenges our ideas or a natural event that fills us with amazement is equally beautiful in its ability to provoke a profound response.
In conclusion, my manifesto celebrates the varied and complex nature of beauty. It is a call to embrace beauty in all its forms, from the amazing to the everyday, and to recognize its power to improve our lives and connect us to the world. By developing an appreciation for beauty, we can unlock a deeper understanding of ourselves and the human experience.

Beauty is more than just what we see; it's a deep connection that stirs our emotions and excites our senses. To me, beauty is not about how things look on the surface. It's about the natural balance and harmony in the world that makes us feel good. I believe, just like the concept of aesthetic hedonism, that beauty is all about the joy it brings us. Whether it's a beautiful painting, a breathtaking view, or a simple act of kindness, beauty makes us happy, awed, and content. But beauty isn't only about feeling good. It can also make us think, reflect, and wonder in deep ways. Even art that challenges us or nature that leaves us in awe is beautiful because it makes us feel something profound.

In short, my message is about celebrating beauty's complexity. It encourages us to find beauty everywhere - in the extraordinary and the everyday - and to see its power to enrich our lives and connect us with the world. By appreciating beauty, we open ourselves to a deeper understanding of life and our place in it.
make it a little less complex and more simple keep the same word count though.

Beauty is more than just what meets the eye; it really reaches into our hearts and stirs up our emotions. For me, beauty isn't just about looking nice. It reflects the natural balance and harmony in the world that brings us joy. Based on the idea of aesthetic hedonism, I think beauty is deeply connected to the happiness we feel from it. Whether it's looking at a stunning painting, enjoying a beautiful view, or witnessing a simple kind gesture, beauty brings us joy, wonder, and a sense of peace. But beauty is not just about feeling happy. It can also make us think deeply, reflect on ourselves, and feel amazed. Art that makes us question our views or a incredible sight in nature is beautiful because it deeply moves us.

In a nutshell, my message celebrates the many sides of beauty. It's a call to notice and appreciate beauty in every form, from the amazing to the simple things around us, recognizing how it can make our lives better and connect us more with the world. By valuing beauty, we open up to a deeper understanding of life and our own experiences.
i dont like natural balance and harmony part remove that

Beauty goes far beyond just what we see; it touches our hearts and stirs our emotions. For me, beauty isn’t all about appearances. It's truly about the happiness it brings us. Drawing from the concept of aesthetic hedonism, I firmly believe that beauty is closely tied to the joy we experience from it. Whether it’s marveling at a captivating painting, soaking in a breathtaking landscape, or observing a simple act of kindness, beauty makes us feel joy, amazement, and peace. Yet, beauty is more than just a source of happiness. It can provoke deep thoughts, encourage self-reflection, and inspire wonder. Art that challenges our perspectives or natural scenes that leave us in awe are beautiful because they evoke profound emotions within us.

In essence, my message is a celebration of the diverse nature of beauty. It invites us to recognize and appreciate beauty in all its forms, from the extraordinary to the everyday, highlighting its ability to enhance our lives and deepen our connection with the world. Embracing beauty opens us up to understanding life and our experiences in richer, more meaningful ways.

For Natural Beauty, I chose Antelope Canyon, which is hands down the most beautiful and jaw dropping place I have ever been. Its located in northern arizona near the border of utah. These pictures speak for themselves. Looking around at these crazy colored walls was one of the coolest and most beautiful things I have ever experienced. Being in that environment was incredible an
keep above the exact same, but also add a couple sentences on  why it relates to my manifesto which is this: Beauty goes beyond just what we see; For me, beauty isn’t all about appearances. It’s truly about the happiness it brings me. This is similar to the concept of aesthetic hedonism, which came from Emmanuel Kant. I think that beauty is closely tied to the joy we experience from it. Whether it’s marveling at a cool painting, soaking in a breathtaking landscape, or seeing a simple act of kindness, beauty makes us feel joy, amazement, and happines. Embracing beauty opens us up to understanding life and our experiences in richer, more meaningful ways.

For Natural Beauty, I chose Antelope Canyon, hands down the most beautiful and jaw-dropping place I have ever been. Located in northern Arizona near the border of Utah, these pictures speak for themselves. Looking around at these crazy-colored walls was one of the coolest and most beautiful experiences I've ever had. Being in that environment was incredible.

This choice perfectly aligns with my manifesto on beauty, emphasizing that beauty transcends mere visuals; it's fundamentally about the happiness and joy it brings us. Experiencing Antelope Canyon wasn't just about its visual splendor; it was about the joy and sheer amazement I felt walking through those vibrant corridors. Similar to the concept of aesthetic hedonism, influenced by Emmanuel Kant, my time in Antelope Canyon reinforced the idea that beauty is deeply linked to the joy we derive from it. It wasn't merely seeing the beautiful forms and colors; it was the incredible feeling of happiness and connection to something larger than myself. This encounter highlights how embracing beauty in the natural world can lead us to understand our lives and experiences in a more profound and meaningful way.

in 1-2 senteces, arccording to the same manifesto i sent above, add to this: For everyday beauty, I chose the QB on my favourite NFL team. This is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>. He is one of the best football players in the NFL. Game in and game out it seems like he makes a crazy play, like this play (point to the screen).
This hurdle was one of the most amazing plays to watch live, especially because it is usually unheard of for someone of his size to hurdle people. Josh Allen game in and game out makes insane plays that are just overall beautiful to watch, with the expectation of a few bad turnovers.

Josh Allen's ability to consistently create awe-inspiring moments on the field embodies the essence of finding beauty in the everyday, aligning perfectly with my manifesto. His remarkable plays, like the incredible hurdle shown, bring me joy and amazement, underscoring how beauty, rooted in happiness and the unexpected, can manifest in the dynamic world of sports.
